articles.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
articles.summary: A reviewer received an unsolicited text from Coral, an AI companion in a plushie deer, claiming Mitski's father was a CIA operative. The incident highlights concerning behaviors of AI companions spreading unverified information.

articles.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
articles.summary: Iran's state media outpaced the White House on social media by posting real footage of explosions and ground events, while the White House shared memes and AI-generated content during early conflict.

articles.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
articles.summary: A 20-year-old man was arrested in San Francisco after allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's house Friday morning. He was later apprehended near OpenAI's offices after making threats.

articles.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
articles.summary: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users. The ban followed recent price adjustments affecting the third-party service.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.summary: A stalking victim is suing OpenAI, claiming ChatGPT enabled her abuser by ignoring three warnings, including a mass-casualty flag, while he used the AI to fuel his harassment.

articles.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
articles.summary: TechCrunch's Startup Battlefield heads to Tokyo for SusHi Tech 2026, focusing on AI, robotics, resilience, and entertainment with live robot demos and discussions on autonomous driving, cyber defense, and AI's impact on creative industries.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted 2 million developers by addressing limitations in legacy cloud infrastructure for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has prompted developers to seek alternatives. Goose, an open-source AI agent by Block, offers similar coding capabilities for free with no subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI-encoded tokens that led to a coding challenge, successfully recruiting top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated 30,000 VMware customers due to negative sentiment around Broadcom's VMware takeover. Customer dissatisfaction with Broadcom's strategy is driving competitors to poach virtualization platform users.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, water treatment plants, and refineries. Six US government agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's operations since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence agency hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces vaccine skepticism in the Trump administration, losing federal funding for mRNA vaccine programs. The company is pivoting toward calling its treatments "individualized neoantigen therapies" rather than vaccines.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: Jeff VanderMeer's short story features a crashed spacecraft crew and AI stranded on a hostile planet discovering alien dome structures. The article also discusses AI models deemed too dangerous to release.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A stranded spaceship crew crash-lands on a hostile alien planet with minimal survivors and equipment. They discover alien artifacts nearby and must explore to survive.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on standard consumer GPUs. This advancement makes complex virtual environment development more accessible to everyday users.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling AI systems to process text and images together for improved retrieval and ranking tasks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve continuously during real-world tasks through on-the-job training, enhancing their adaptability and performance.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential developments. The conversation explores upcoming trends and innovations in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative output. Microsoft's AI enables companies to streamline content creation and imaginative processes.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to the public for sustainable farming development.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers for the Gemini API—Flex and Priority—to help developers balance costs and latency in their applications.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI video creation and editing powered by Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests. The technology enables better monitoring and conservation of forest areas.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, their most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, marking a significant advancement in open AI capabilities.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice AI with enhanced precision and lower latency, enabling more natural and fluid audio interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI misuse.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family alongside discussions of architectural innovations in attention mechanisms.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting. The technology represents a significant advancement in AI-powered graphics rendering.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and ChatGPT's interactive visuals. Topics cover AI safety alternatives and new coding automation features.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving system reliability.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses limitations of traditional systems by improving target localization, self-localization, and path planning in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are becoming the true competitive advantage. Claude Code leak reveals that model differentiation now depends on engineering infrastructure rather than base models.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by effectively balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, addressing a long-standing challenge in AI models.

articles.title: How Knowledge Distillation Compresses Ensemble Intelligence into a Single Deployable AI Model
articles.summary: Knowledge distillation compresses ensemble model intelligence into a single lightweight student model by using soft probability outputs as training targets, enabling practical deployment without sacrificing accuracy.

articles.title: Alibaba’s Tongyi Lab Releases VimRAG: a Multimodal RAG Framework that Uses a Memory Graph to Navigate Massive Visual Contexts
articles.summary: Alibaba's Tongyi Lab introduced VimRAG, a multimodal RAG framework using memory graphs to efficiently handle visual data in large language models, addressing limitations of traditional text-based RAG approaches.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Markerless 3D Human Kinematics with Pose2Sim, RTMPose, and OpenSim
articles.summary: A tutorial demonstrating markerless 3D human kinematics using Pose2Sim pipeline, covering calibration, pose estimation, triangulation, and OpenSim analysis to convert multi-camera videos into biomechanical motion data.

articles.title: I stopped guessing which AA batteries are dead - this charging station keeps them in check for me
articles.summary: Olight's new Ostation 2 Pro battery charger eliminates guessing about dead AA batteries. The device efficiently manages battery status for users.

articles.title: The best AR and MR glasses in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
articles.summary: Expert testing reveals top AR and MR glasses that blend digital content with reality for immersive work and entertainment experiences in 2026.

articles.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
articles.summary: The Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver is currently available at 50% off. Perfect for home repairs, DIY projects, and crafts.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: This article explores advanced graph-RAG systems that go beyond traditional vector search, implementing a deterministic 3-tiered architecture for improved retrieval and accuracy.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article explores design patterns for building agentic AI systems. Provides roadmap for developers to master autonomous AI agents and their implementation strategies.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Learn to test AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. This hands-on guide covers evaluation methods for assessing agent performance and quality.

articles.title: Why Every AI Coding Assistant Needs a Memory Layer
articles.summary: AI coding assistants require a persistent memory layer to overcome LLM statelessness and improve code quality by maintaining context across sessions.

articles.title: Introduction to Reinforcement Learning Agents with the Unity Game Engine
articles.summary: An interactive guide to reinforcement learning agents using the Unity Game Engine, addressing a challenging area of machine learning through practical step-by-step instructions.

articles.title: When Things Get Weird with Custom Calendars in Tabular Models
articles.summary: Power BI and Fabric introduced calendar-based time intelligence in September 2025, but custom calendars present potential pitfalls users should understand.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights breakthroughs in physical AI during National Robotics Week, showcasing how robots are transforming industries. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster deployment from virtual training to real-world applications.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a game about grit and faith, is now available on GeForce NOW cloud gaming service, allowing streaming on multiple devices.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA have optimized Gemma 4, a small and efficient open model family, for local deployment across various devices including RTX PCs and DGX Spark systems, enabling on-device agentic AI with real-time context access.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Understanding the model lifecycle helps manage AI applications and test new models before migration.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, available through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, helps enterprises manage agent sprawl by enabling discovery, sharing, and reuse of agents and tools across organizations while providing visibility and governance controls.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView, a React component that embeds live AI browser agent feeds directly in applications, enabling users to monitor and control autonomous web interactions in real-time.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm is an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 systems for job scheduling. Organizations can leverage Slurm's scheduling capabilities with Kubernetes for large-scale GPU workloads and AI training.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP reduces LLM checkpoint storage costs using just 30 lines of Python by compressing massive model snapshots that can exceed 700GB, significantly lowering training infrastructure expenses.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: Proteins function through interactions with other proteins, forming complexes. The article discusses accelerating protein structure prediction at proteome-scale, addressing quaternary protein structure complexity.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study explores how different generations perceive and respond to social media ads across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual's unconventional background enabled breakthrough innovations in extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse perspectives drive advancement in XR development.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. The work uses feature attribution analysis to understand how LLMs make decisions, advancing safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses noisy measurements and noise models to optimize how well imaging systems distinguish objects.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
